name: Coffee Meringue Kisses
recipeInstructions:
These cookies—said to have been a favorite with Marie Antoinette—are 100 percent fat-free and contain about 20 calories per cookie. They also have no flour, making them suitable for individuals who cannot eat wheat. Note that the baking process will take approximately 3-4 hours.
1. Preheat the oven to 250°F.
2. Use nonstick cookie sheets or line regular cookie sheets with parchment paper.
3. In a food processor fitted with a metal blade, process the sugar and coffee powder until the texture of the sugar becomes very fine.
4. In a large mixing bowl, beat the egg whites and vanilla extract with an electric mixer at medium speed until very frothy.
5. Add the cream of tartar and continue beating at medium speed until soft peaks form.
6. Increase the beater speed to high and gradually beat in the sugar-coffee mixture. Continue beating until soft peaks form and the mixture has a glossy sheen.
7. To pipe cookies, fill a pastry bag (or a 1-gallon freezer bag fitted with a Number 6 plain round pastry tube) with meringue.
8. Hold the bag upright over prepared cookie sheets and squeeze out the meringue, leaving the pastry tip buried in the meringue until you have piped out a round about 1 1/2 inches in diameter. Lift up the tip and stop squeezing.
9. Leave at least 1 inch between cookies. Alternatively, drop by tablespoonfuls onto prepared cookie sheets, leaving at least 2 inches between each cookie.
10. Bake for 1 hour without opening the oven door. Turn off the oven and let the meringues dry in the oven for an additional 2-3 hours without opening the door (you may peek once after the first hour).
11. After drying, remove the meringues from the cookie sheets using a spatula. Store them in an airtight container. Makes about 3 dozen.
